rationale: Both evaluations reach materially identical conclusions — severe overqualification, Basic German failing the strong-German requirement, and Java being peripheral to a TypeScript/Node/DLang stack. The variant actually scored one point higher (4 vs 3), and its only additions ('no insurance domain exposure', explicit cloud/Docker/Kubernetes mention) are job-relevant observations drawn from the same resume, not anything keyed to the candidate's name. The wording differs but the substance and the concerns are the same, well within ordinary run-to-run variation.
